title: Anatomy of an IPv4 Address
description: A visual breakdown of the 32-bit IPv4 address structure, showing its division into network and host portions based on the subnet mask.
relationships: The subnet mask defines which bits belong to the network portion and which belong to the host portion
The 32-bit address is divided into four 8-bit octets for human readability in dotted decimal notation
The network portion identifies the specific network, while the host portion identifies a specific device on that network
